_description: Standalone actuarial model for the Italy Post-TKR Rehabilitation Plan (italy-tkr-rehab-2026). All monetary amounts in EUR (1 unit = €1.00). Sources: pazienti.it, vannistrigelli.com, prontopro.it, tiassisto.it, quotidianosanita.it (2025-2026 Italian market rates).

clinicalContext.indication: Total Knee Replacement (TKR / PTG — Protesi Totale di Ginocchio)
icd10Codes: Z96.651 (presence of right artificial knee joint)
Z96.652 (presence of left artificial knee joint)
clinicalContext.clinicalDifferenceVsTHR: TKR rehab emphasises ROM recovery (target: flexion ≥ 110°, extension deficit ≤ 5°) and quadriceps re-education more intensively than THR. Oedema management (cryotherapy, compression) is prominent in the first 2-3 weeks. Sessions are slightly longer and more hands-on, justifying a higher per-session rate (€75 vs €70 for THR). The risk of stiffness (arthrofibrosis) if ROM targets are not met by day 21 requires closer physiotherapy monitoring.

sources: guidamed.it — TKR cost and recovery overview
prontopro.it / cronoshare.it — 2026 domiciliary physio market rates
tiassisto.it — 2026 RSA/rehab facility daily rates (North-Centre Italy)

phase1_hospital.label: Surgical episode + 7-day inpatient stay
includes: Knee prosthesis (standard cemented or uncemented, primary)
Initial inpatient physiotherapy (CPM machine + 3-4 manual sessions)
phase1_hospital.rationale: Mid-range private clinic, North-Centre Italy, standard cemented prosthesis. Slightly less than THR (18000) due to lower prosthesis cost and comparable OR complexity.

phase2_rehabFacility.label: Inpatient rehabilitation facility
includes: Daily physiotherapy sessions (60-90 min): ROM exercises, quadriceps strengthening, gait training
Orthopaedic follow-up at day 21 and day 35
phase2_rehabFacility.rationale: Same facility type and daily rate as THR (€130/day x 35 days). Knee rehab at this phase is equally intensive.

phase3_homeRehab.label: Home physiotherapy
includes: Final outcome assessment (Oxford Knee Score + ROM measurement)
phase3_homeRehab.rationale: 18 sessions at €75 vs 16 sessions at €70 for THR. TKR requires 2 extra sessions due to more intensive ROM work and higher stiffness risk. Rate €75 reflects slightly more hands-on manual therapy per session.

comparisonWithTHR.note: TKR is 7.5% less expensive than THR due to lower prosthesis cost. Rehab facility cost is identical; home physio is slightly higher due to extra sessions.

rationale.utilizationRate: TKR incidence in 55-75 age cohort is ~1-2% per year, similar to THR. Pool targets members post-diagnosis or post-surgical indication. 5% is conservative for enrolled at-risk population.
rationale.bufferMargin: 20% reserve buffer covers: claim processing delays, partial denials, exceptional cases above mean cost, operational overhead.
rationale.poolSize: Minimum viable pool for meaningful risk diversification. At 5% utilization with n=100, Poisson variance = sqrt(5) ≈ 2.2.

comparisonWithTHR.note: TKR premiums are 7-8% lower than THR at all tiers, reflecting the lower per-claim cost.

sponsorSeedFunding.description: One-time sponsor contribution covering ~30% of expected annual payout per tier.

lpBackstop.description: Liquidity backstop for variance above expected utilization. Covers ~2 additional full-100% TKR claims beyond the expected 5.
lpBackstop.rationale: 2 x 21900 = 43800. Slightly smaller than THR backstop (50000) because TKR per-claim cost is lower.

primaryOutcome.note: Normalized: OKS 34/48 = 70.8% → healthAlphaScore 7100. Lower than THR (HHS 80 = healthAlphaScore 8000) reflecting that knee scores are harder to achieve post-arthroplasty.

secondaryOutcomes.passingValue: able to climb 1 flight without handrail

earlyWarningThreshold.action: Oracle triggers alert; physiotherapist must increase session frequency or refer for manipulation under anaesthesia

oracleAttestation.secondaryOracle: Optional: wearable device (ORACLE_TYPE_WEARABLE_DATA_PROVIDER) attesting gait metrics and step count
oracleAttestation.quorum: 1-of-1 (single physio) or 2-of-2 (physio + wearable) if wearable enrolled
oracleAttestation.evidencePolicy: All clinical data off-chain. Only blake3 hashes on-chain. GDPR compliant.

bondMechanic.purpose: Adherence incentive. Refunded to patient if OKS >= 34 at day 90. Redistributed to pool if patient abandons protocol.

sensitivityAnalysis.description: Impact of varying utilization rate on premium sustainability at standard-70% tier

scenarios.note: Premium becomes unaffordable — pool requires sponsor subsidy or LP backstop expansion

crossPlanComparison.description: Side-by-side summary of THR vs TKR plans on the devnet
